text.title: Go → TypeScript DTO Contract via tygo Core Rules
text.summary: ≥5 testable rules pinning the methodology's preconditions, output shape, and skip path. Each rule pairs a binary check with rationale + named source.

rule.statement: Go DTO structs are the single source of truth; TypeScript types are generated by tygo, never hand-written.
rule.rationale: Two sources of truth diverge silently; single source eliminates drift.

rule.statement: tygo.yaml MUST be committed to the repo with explicit `packages`, `frontmatter`, and `type_mappings`.
rule.rationale: Implicit config breaks reproducibility across dev machines.

rule.statement: CI MUST run `tygo generate && git diff --exit-code` and fail on uncommitted regeneration.
rule.rationale: Without the gate, Go-side changes ship without TS regen and the frontend breaks at runtime.

rule.statement: Generated TS lives under a known directory (e.g. frontend/src/api-types/) prefixed with `// Code generated by tygo. DO NOT EDIT.` header.
rule.rationale: Locating + warning prevents accidental hand-edits and code review confusion.

rule.statement: Non-trivial Go types (time.Time, decimal.Decimal, json.RawMessage) MUST have explicit `type_mappings` to TS equivalents.
rule.rationale: Default mappings produce `any` or `string` and lose type safety.

rule.statement: If gRPC + protoc already covers the surface OR DTO churn is daily during prototype OR frontend isn't TS, STOP.
rule.rationale: tygo overlapping with protoc or churn-heavy phase wastes cycles.
